title: Example run of MixedUpParents
text: diagnostic markers first converted ancestry copy-number segments. ancestral_n_segments() finds stretches chromosome individual number copies diagnostic ancestry. notation paper, segments empirical version ancestry tracts 𝒯w\mathscr{T}_w individual ww. particular segment ss, ancestry state written 𝒯w(s)\mathscr{T}_w^{(s)}. step, extension step follows, slowest parts workflow. full simulated data set can take several minutes. extension step also requires bedtools available shell path. next step combines ancestry-specific copy-number segments full ancestry states. diploid individual three possible ancestries, possible states include values A2B0C0, A1B1C0, A0B2C0. labels correspond paper’s AxByCzA_xB_yC_z notation 𝒯w(s)\mathscr{T}_w^{(s)}. example, A1B1C0 means , segment, individual carries one copy ancestry , one copy ancestry B, copies ancestry C. biological labels ordered WCT, RBT, YCT. ordering defines internal labels , B, C. Thus, object E main bridge observed diagnostic marker data ancestry-tract notation paper. row gives one segment ss one individual, chromosome, start stop positions, copy-number state 𝒯w(s)\mathscr{T}_w^{(s)}. extended segments useful diagnostic objects right. first plot summarizes fraction individual’s genome assigned full ancestry state. black line shows admixture fraction ancestry , WCT example.  second plot shows ancestry states laid along genome. often good way spot unusual individuals regions diagnostic markers sparse.
code: AS <- ancestral_n_segments(   D = TW$spp_diag_snps,   chrom_levels = unique(TW$spp_diag_snps$chrom) )  AS #> # A tibble: 352,253 × 8 #>    diag_spp indiv chrom chrom_f     n n_f      start     stop #>    <chr>    <chr> <chr> <fct>   <int> <fct>    <int>    <int> #>  1 WCT      30056 omy01 omy01       1 1      5604476 74803921 #>  2 WCT      30056 omy02 omy02       2 2     45656675 85467553 #>  3 WCT      30056 omy03 omy03       2 2     53419215 64080711 #>  4 WCT      30056 omy04 omy04       2 2     17200856 65959628 #>  5 WCT      30056 omy05 omy05       2 2     26549554 71264380 #>  6 WCT      30056 omy06 omy06       1 1      6207101 58538807 #>  7 WCT      30056 omy07 omy07       1 1     17341546 58214623 #>  8 WCT      30056 omy08 omy08       2 2     14525752 69379276 #>  9 WCT      30056 omy09 omy09       2 2     27468641 68137677 #> 10 WCT      30056 omy10 omy10       2 2     26820894 50560803 #> # ℹ 352,243 more rows species_levels <- c(TW$spp_names, "YCT")  E <- extend_ancestral_segments_using_bedtools(   AS,   species_levels = species_levels ) #> Extending ancestral segments using bedtools in temp directory: /var/folders/ln/5brnn1wx0vj58fhlbbdjm8h40000gp/T//RtmpuxF5iu/file1093646a48430  E #> # A tibble: 116,798 × 5 #>    indiv chrom_f copy_num    start     stop #>    <chr> <fct>   <chr>       <dbl>    <dbl> #>  1 23939 omy01   A2B0C0    5604475 76705842 #>  2 23939 omy02   A2B0C0   14627479 85467554 #>  3 23939 omy03   A2B0C0   16863659 70168237 #>  4 23939 omy04   A2B0C0   17200854 75324433 #>  5 23939 omy05   A2B0C0   26549553 71264381 #>  6 23939 omy06   A2B0C0     869438 58538808 #>  7 23939 omy07   A1B1C0   16963823 58933104 #>  8 23939 omy08   A1B1C0   11401961 66452476 #>  9 23939 omy09   A2B0C0   32804922 68137678 #> 10 23939 omy10   A2B0C0   10179164 56110002 #> # ℹ 116,788 more rows ancestry_barplot(E) ancestry_barplot(E, plot_type = "segments")

title: Example run of MixedUpParents
text: pairwise_genotype_probs() uses diagnostic variable markers. can call , prepare_for_gpp() estimates allele frequencies ancestry background, estimates individual admixture fractions extended ancestry segments, builds integer-indexed data structures faster C++ code. paper’s notation, diagnostic variable markers segment ss sets D(s)D^{(s)} V(s)V^{(s)}. corresponding allele-frequency matrices 𝐏̂D(s)\mathbf{\hat{P}}_D^{(s)} 𝐏̂V(s)\mathbf{\hat{P}}_V^{(s)}, estimated admixture fractions individual 𝐐̃w\tilde{\mathbf{Q}}_w. One detail important: extended ancestry segments encoded , B, C, diagnostic marker labels must use internal labels. conversion preserves ancestry ordering used . Now can prepare object passed pairwise_genotype_probs(). example, PRP$AF contains estimated allele frequencies variable diagnostic markers ancestry backgrounds, PRP$AD contains estimated admixture fractions individual. correspond paper’s 𝐏̂D\mathbf{\hat{P}}_D, 𝐏̂V\mathbf{\hat{P}}_V, 𝐐̃\tilde{\mathbf{Q}} terms. package stores form can indexed efficiently comparing candidate offspring kk candidate parent pp.
code: new_diag <- TW$spp_diag_snps %>%   mutate(     ancestry_index = as.integer(factor(diag_spp, levels = species_levels)),     diag_spp = c("A", "B", "C")[ancestry_index]   ) %>%   select(diag_spp, chrom, pos, indiv, n)  new_diag %>%   distinct(diag_spp, chrom, pos) %>%   count(diag_spp, name = "diagnostic_markers") #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   diag_spp diagnostic_markers #>   <chr>                 <int> #> 1 A                       245 #> 2 B                       813 #> 3 C                       239 PRP <- prepare_for_gpp(   V = TW$variable_snps,   M = new_diag,   E = E,   diag_err_rate = 0.005 )  names(PRP) #> [1] "mKey"      "AFmat"     "ADmat"     "isDiagVec" "AF"        "AD"        #> [7] "Genos"     "ir_E" PRP$AF %>%   slice_head(n = 6) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>    cIdx     pos  mIdx isDiag     A      B     C #>   <int>   <int> <int>  <int> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1     0 5604476     0      1 0.995 0.005  0.005 #> 2     0 5604516     1      1 0.005 0.995  0.005 #> 3     0 5604542     2      0 0.994 0.0439 0.5   #> 4     0 5604546     3      1 0.005 0.005  0.995 #> 5     0 5604556     4      1 0.995 0.005  0.005 #> 6     0 8362156     5      1 0.005 0.995  0.005  PRP$AD %>%   slice_head(n = 6) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   indiv  iIdx     A      B     C #>   <chr> <int> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 23939     0 0.911 0.0894     0 #> 2 23940     1 0.835 0.165      0 #> 3 23943     2 0.907 0.0928     0 #> 4 23945     3 0.978 0.0222     0 #> 5 23946     4 0.979 0.0209     0 #> 6 23947     5 1     0          0

title: Example run of MixedUpParents
text: empirical analysis, candidate offspring candidate parents usually come sampling dates, ages, cohorts, metadata. simulated data set, use true simulation generation construct candidate sets. Final-generation individuals treated candidate offspring, sampled individuals treated candidate parents. pedigree table two rows per individual, one ancestry-specific admixture fraction. keep one row per individual metadata joins. run pairwise probability calculation final-generation offspring. chunk computationally intensive; threads object set setup chunk controls number parallel workers used parallel::mclapply(). ordered pair, pairwise_genotype_probs() intersects ancestry segments candidate parent offspring. gives segment set s∈p∩ks \p \cap k used Equations 1 2 paper: Equation 1 (eq:L0) defines L0(k∣p)L_0(k \mid p), conditional probability offspring data unrelated hypothesis, Equation 2 (eq:L1) defines L1(k∣p)L_1(k \mid p), corresponding probability parent-offspring hypothesis. column logl_ratio log-likelihood parent-offspring model minus log-likelihood unrelated model. Larger values indicate stronger support candidate parent-offspring relationship. notation paper, Λk,pPO/U=log⁡(L1(k∣p)L0(k∣p)). \Lambda_{k,p}^{\mathrm{PO/U}} = \log\left(\frac{L_1(k \mid p)}{L_0(k \mid p)}\right).  package returns log probabilities, code calculation probKidParental - probKidUnrel exactly log-likelihood ratio.
code: qped <- TW$sampled_pedQ %>%   filter(anc_pop == 1)  kids <- qped %>%   filter(ind_time == 0) %>%   pull(ped_id) %>%   unique()  all_candi <- qped %>%   pull(ped_id) %>%   unique()  tibble(   candidate_offspring = length(kids),   candidate_parents = length(all_candi) ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   candidate_offspring candidate_parents #>                 <int>             <int> #> 1                1186              3576 run_mup <- function(kids, pars, L) {   parallel::mclapply(     kids,     function(kid) {       candi <- setdiff(pars, kid)        pairwise_genotype_probs(         L = L,         kid = kid,         par = candi       )     },     mc.cores = threads   ) %>%     bind_rows() }  if(system2("hostname", stdout = TRUE)[1] == "Erics-BGP-Laptop.local") {   # For local vignette rendering while editing, eric uses the precomputed result.   # This code should not run on anyone else's computer.  Instead, it will run   # run_mup() for a long time!   logls <- read_rds("../mup_logls.rds") } else {   logls <- run_mup(kids, all_candi, PRP) %>%     mutate(logl_ratio = probKidParental - probKidUnrel) %>%     arrange(desc(logl_ratio)) }   logls %>%   select(kid_id, par_id, probKidUnrel, probKidParental, logl_ratio,          nonMissingDiag, nonMissingVar) %>%   slice_head(n = 10) #> # A tibble: 10 × 7 #>    kid_id par_id probKidUnrel probKidParental logl_ratio nonMissingDiag #>    <chr>  <chr>         <dbl>           <dbl>      <dbl>          <int> #>  1 30407  30409         -301.           -145.      156.             615 #>  2 30407  30411         -297.           -160.      136.             594 #>  3 30571  27155         -300.           -187.      114.             616 #>  4 30407  30408         -246.           -148.       98.1            599 #>  5 30571  24490         -307.           -215.       91.9            644 #>  6 30407  29807         -334.           -251.       82.5            619 #>  7 30407  30405         -253.           -175.       77.8            598 #>  8 31669  28049         -197.           -120.       77.3            624 #>  9 30571  28362         -308.           -236.       72.1            608 #> 10 32862  29721         -216.           -153.       63.5            609 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: nonMissingVar <int> ggplot(logls, aes(x = logl_ratio)) +   geom_histogram(bins = 60) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "red", linewidth = 0.25) +   xlab("log likelihood ratio: parent-offspring minus unrelated") +   ylab("candidate pairs") +   theme_bw()

title: Example run of MixedUpParents
text: package require pedigree. , though, simulation truth lets us check whether highest-scoring candidate pairs real relatives. First record whether offspring’s true parents included among sampled candidates. Next attach simulation metadata true pairwise relationship labels. join interpretation simulated example; part required MixedUpParents workflow. simulated data, can also look likelihood-ratio distributions true relationship class. relationship labels come pedigree simulation, MixedUpParents. relationship classes subdivided max_hit column: example, Si-1 Si-2 sibling relationships one two major shared ancestors, corresponding roughly half siblings full siblings. use convention FC, display class , yielding labels -1, -2, FC-1, FC-2. label PO denotes true parent-offspring pairs, U denotes pairs appear simulated relationship table. simulated relationships max_hit greater 2; omit rare categories plots .  C++ calculation also returns log likelihood full-sibling model. Rather plotting quantity every relationship class, useful focus two classes parent-offspring full-sibling models meant distinguish. plot , Si-2 denotes simulated full siblings, Si-1 denotes half siblings, -2 denotes one close ancestral relationship classes, PO denotes simulated parent-offspring pairs. x-axis parent-offspring vs. unrelated log-likelihood ratio, log⁡(L1(k∣p)/L0(k∣p))\log(L_1(k \mid p) / L_0(k \mid p)). y-axis parent-offspring vs. full-sibling log-likelihood ratio, log⁡(L1(k∣p)/LFS(k∣p))\log(L_1(k \mid p) / L_\mathrm{FS}(k \mid p)). common screening rule discard candidate parent-offspring pairs full-sibling model likely parent-offspring model. paper’s terms log⁡(LFS/L1)>0\log(L_\mathrm{FS}/L_1) > 0, equivalent plotted quantity log⁡(L1/LFS)<0\log(L_1/L_\mathrm{FS}) < 0. Thus, points horizontal zero line treated possible full-sibling pairs rather retained parent-offspring calls.  compact summary, keep top-ranked candidate parents offspring. larger development workflow tables written disk downstream ROC analyses. vignette clearer print relevant table directly. key columns final table kid_id, par_id, logl_ratio, po_fs_logl_ratio. rel_label, dom_relat, max_hit, ped_p1, ped_p2 columns come simulation included help verify example. empirical analysis, candidate pairs large log-likelihood ratios carried forward whatever downstream filtering, ranking, manual review appropriate study design.
code: pars_in_samples <- qped %>%   filter(ped_id %in% kids) %>%   select(ped_id, ped_p1, ped_p2) %>%   mutate(     p1_in_sample = ped_p1 %in% all_candi,     p2_in_sample = ped_p2 %in% all_candi,     num_parents_in_sample = p1_in_sample + p2_in_sample   ) %>%   mutate(     across(c(ped_id, ped_p1, ped_p2), as.character)   )  pars_in_samples #> # A tibble: 1,186 × 6 #>    ped_id ped_p1 ped_p2 p1_in_sample p2_in_sample num_parents_in_sample #>    <chr>  <chr>  <chr>  <lgl>        <lgl>                        <int> #>  1 30056  27578  27571  TRUE         FALSE                            1 #>  2 30059  27578  27571  TRUE         FALSE                            1 #>  3 32119  28770  28832  TRUE         FALSE                            1 #>  4 30061  27578  27571  TRUE         FALSE                            1 #>  5 33004  29998  29377  TRUE         FALSE                            1 #>  6 30068  27788  27453  TRUE         TRUE                             2 #>  7 30070  27788  27453  TRUE         TRUE                             2 #>  8 30071  27788  27453  TRUE         TRUE                             2 #>  9 30072  27788  27453  TRUE         TRUE                             2 #> 10 30074  27731  27484  FALSE        FALSE                            0 #> # ℹ 1,176 more rows meta <- qped %>%   select(ped_id, ind_pop, ind_time, admix_fract) %>%   rename(id = ped_id, pop = ind_pop, time = ind_time, q1 = admix_fract) %>%   mutate(id = as.character(id))  kid_meta <- meta names(kid_meta) <- str_c("kid", names(meta), sep = "_")  par_meta <- meta names(par_meta) <- str_c("par", names(meta), sep = "_")  all_pairs <- logls %>%   left_join(kid_meta, by = join_by(kid_id)) %>%   left_join(par_meta, by = join_by(par_id)) %>%   mutate(     min_id = as.character(pmin(as.integer(kid_id), as.integer(par_id))),     max_id = as.character(pmax(as.integer(kid_id), as.integer(par_id)))   ) %>%   left_join(     TW$pairwise_relats %>%       select(id_1, id_2, dom_relat, max_hit),     by = join_by(min_id == id_1, max_id == id_2)   ) %>%   select(-min_id, -max_id) %>%   mutate(     dom_relat = replace_na(dom_relat, "U"),     max_hit = replace_na(max_hit, 0L),     rel_label = case_when(       dom_relat == "A" ~ str_c("AN-", max_hit),       dom_relat %in% c("Si", "FC") ~ str_c(dom_relat, "-", max_hit),       TRUE ~ dom_relat     ),     po_fs_logl_ratio = probKidParental - probKidFullSib,     po_fs_support = if_else(       po_fs_logl_ratio > 0,       "PO/FS > 0",       "PO/FS <= 0"     )   )  all_pairs %>%   select(kid_id, par_id, logl_ratio, po_fs_logl_ratio, rel_label,          dom_relat, max_hit,          kid_time, par_time, kid_q1, par_q1) %>%   slice_head(n = 10) #> # A tibble: 10 × 11 #>    kid_id par_id logl_ratio po_fs_logl_ratio rel_label dom_relat max_hit #>    <chr>  <chr>       <dbl>            <dbl> <chr>     <chr>       <int> #>  1 30407  30409       156.            -12.6  Si-2      Si              2 #>  2 30407  30411       136.            -16.3  Si-2      Si              2 #>  3 30571  27155       114.            -13.7  AN-2      A               2 #>  4 30407  30408        98.1           -18.6  Si-2      Si              2 #>  5 30571  24490        91.9           -14.4  U         U               0 #>  6 30407  29807        82.5            -8.26 AN-2      A               2 #>  7 30407  30405        77.8           -17.2  Si-2      Si              2 #>  8 31669  28049        77.3             9.04 PO        PO              1 #>  9 30571  28362        72.1           -32.2  U         U               0 #> 10 32862  29721        63.5             6.09 PO        PO              1 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: kid_time <dbl>, par_time <dbl>, kid_q1 <dbl>, #> #   par_q1 <dbl>  plot_pairs <- all_pairs %>%   filter(!(dom_relat %in% c("A", "Si", "FC") & max_hit > 2)) ggplot(plot_pairs, aes(x = logl_ratio)) +   geom_histogram(bins = 80) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "red", linewidth = 0.25) +   facet_wrap(~ rel_label, scales = "free") +   xlab("log likelihood ratio: parent-offspring minus unrelated") +   ylab("candidate pairs") +   theme_bw() all_pairs %>%   filter(rel_label %in% c("Si-1", "Si-2", "AN-2", "PO")) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = logl_ratio, y = po_fs_logl_ratio, colour = po_fs_support)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +   geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +   facet_wrap(~ rel_label, scales = "free") +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("PO/FS > 0" = "#1b9e77", "PO/FS <= 0" = "#7570b3"),     name = "PO vs. FS support"   ) +   xlab("log likelihood ratio: parent-offspring minus unrelated") +   ylab("log likelihood ratio: parent-offspring minus full sibling") +   theme_bw() top_pairs <- all_pairs %>%   arrange(kid_id, desc(logl_ratio)) %>%   group_by(kid_id) %>%   slice_head(n = 5) %>%   ungroup() %>%   left_join(     pars_in_samples,     by = join_by(kid_id == ped_id),     relationship = "many-to-one"   )  top_pairs %>%   select(kid_id, par_id, logl_ratio, po_fs_logl_ratio, rel_label,          dom_relat, max_hit,          ped_p1, ped_p2, num_parents_in_sample,          nonMissingDiag, nonMissingVar) #> # A tibble: 5,930 × 12 #>    kid_id par_id logl_ratio po_fs_logl_ratio rel_label dom_relat max_hit ped_p1 #>    <chr>  <chr>       <dbl>            <dbl> <chr>     <chr>       <int> <chr>  #>  1 30056  27578      35.3               7.67 PO        PO              1 27578  #>  2 30056  30061      25.6              -9.10 Si-2      Si              2 27578  #>  3 30056  30059      -0.608           -22.6  Si-2      Si              2 27578  #>  4 30056  25119      -3.85             -2.71 U         U               0 27578  #>  5 30056  30623      -3.86             -2.18 U         U               0 27578  #>  6 30059  27578      32.9               9.34 PO        PO              1 27578  #>  7 30059  30061      15.4             -32.7  Si-2      Si              2 27578  #>  8 30059  30240       5.71             -3.17 Si-1      Si              1 27578  #>  9 30059  30056       1.19            -18.6  Si-2      Si              2 27578  #> 10 30059  25298      -0.130             3.24 U         U               0 27578  #> # ℹ 5,920 more rows #> # ℹ 4 more variables: ped_p2 <chr>, num_parents_in_sample <int>, #> #   nonMissingDiag <int>, nonMissingVar <int>

code: ancestry_barplot(   E,   plot_type = "fracts",   diagnostic_markers = NULL,   variable_markers = NULL,   inds_per_row = 400,   sort_spec = c("desc(QA)"),   admixture_line = "A",   catOrd = c("A2B0C0", "A1B1C0", "A1B0C1", "A0B1C1", "A0B0C2", "A0B2C0"),   fill_scale = scale_fill_brewer(type = "div", palette = "RdYlBu") )

title: Very simple summaries of extended ancestry segments — ancestry_barplot
text: E tibble extended ancestry segments. format output extend_ancestral_segments_3() plot_type string saying type plot make: "fracts" just plots overall fractions ancestry copy-numbers individual, "segments" actually places ancestry copy-number segments along vertical axis according genome. diagnostic_markers tibble species-diagnostic markers.  Must column diag_spp holds value , B, C, chrom, pos, indiv, n.  n number copies species diagnostic allele locus.  Obviously chromosome names sample names must match E. variable_markers tibble holds variable markers. Must column var_spp holds value , B, C, naming species marker known variable. inds_per_row Number indviuals row plot sort_spec string input arrange() order sort individuals certain ways.  default admixture proportion "" species, .e. QA.  use QB QC, , wanted sort fractions different full ancestry patterns, use, example: sort_spec <- c("desc(A2B0C0)", "desc(A1B1C0)", "desc(A1B0C1)", "desc(A0B2C0)", "desc(A0B1C1)", "desc(A0B0C2)"). , probably best just sort admixture fractions. way admixture fraction line gets plotted reasonable. admixture_line string giving Q value plot line overlaps columns.  , B, C.  NULL, null line plotted showing admixture fraction. catOrd order want colors different full ancestry pattern categories appear columns. fill_scale desired fill scale.  Uses color brewer palette default

Calculate the genotype probabilities for pairs of individuals — pairwise_genotype_probs
text: L list output prepare_for_gpp(). kid ID individual (actual ID, iIdx) treated kid. par vector actual IDs individuals treated parents debug integer flag. debug1 = 0 means return debug information. Otherwise function returns list lot extra information can used verify calculations done correctly. debug = 1, information returned rate one row per segment, genotype information gets returned.  debug = 2, information returned one row per marker, includes genotype probabilities marker unrelated case. debug = 3 information returned one row marker distinct value ancestry haplotype segregated parent. (allows us check calculations probability genotypes given ancestries segregated haplotype). NOTE: debug > 1 runs pretty slowly many push_back calls.  using , best pass farly small data sets (.e. one candidate kid compare handful candidate parents).

text: IXG integer matrix intersected intervals columns kIdx: 0-based index kid pIdx: 0-based index candidate parent cIdx: 0-based index chromosome anck: ternary trit giving ancestry kid segment ancp: ternary trit giving ancestry candidate parent segment start: integer base pair start segment. probably used just know reaching new segment. stop: integer base pair stop segment (probably used) mIdx: 0-based index marker pos:  base-pair position marker (likely used) gk:   count 1 alleles kid genotype (0, 1, 2, -1 missing data) gp:   count 1 alleles parent genotype (0, 1, 2, -1 missing data) AF numeric matrix number columns equal number ancestries ordered like , B, C,... number rows equal number variable diagnostic markers combined. isD integer vector 0s 1s length equal number variable diagnostic markers combined. 1 means marker species-diagnostic marker 0 means otherwise. AD numeric matrix number columns equal number ancestries number rows equal total number individuals got put integer representation. row sums one.  admixture fractions. debug IntegerVector, use first element.  debug(0) == 0 means debug information. Otherwise function returns list lot extra information can used verify calculations done correctly. debug(0) == 1, information returned rate one row per segment, genotype information gets returned.  debug(0) == 2, information returned one row per marker, includes genotype probabilities marker unrelated case. debug(0) == 3 information returned one row marker distinct value ancestry segregated haplotype.

SF fraction chromosome's vertical space occupied species specific markers segments (opposed blank break chromosomes) MOP "marker overhang part", fraction vertical space devoted markers segments accounted marker "whisker" extends either top bottom segment E output extend_ancestral_segments_3() want plot well. title: Make a plot of the ancestral segments with 0, 1, or 2 copies of a species-specific allele — plot_ancestral_segs
code: data(diag_markers_10_fish) M <- diag_markers_10_fish D <- ancestral_n_segments(M, unique(M$chrom)) g <- plot_ancestral_segs(D, M)  # with many individuals, it is recommended to plot this large: # like: ggsave(g, filename = "gplot.pdf", width = 30, height = 40)  # If you want to add the extended segments to it: # E <- extend_ancestral_segments_3(D, c("WCT", "RBT", "YCT")) # g2 <- plot_ancestral_segs(D, M, E = E)  #' # with many individuals, it is recommended to plot this large: # like: ggsave(g2, filename = "E-gplot.pdf", width = 30, height = 40)
